Title: Expression of the MYB transcription factor gene BplMYB46 affects abiotic stress tolerance and secondary cell wall deposition in Betula platyphylla

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall formation and wood properties; osmoprotection under abiotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem xylem/fibres for lignin, cellulose and hemicellulose; whole seedlings/leaves under stress for proline
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi silencing; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; promoter-reporter transient assay; physiological/metabolite assays; histochemical staining
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves salt and osmotic stress tolerance; increases lignin and cellulose content; decreases hemicellulose content; reduces water loss
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator coordinating abiotic stress-responsive metabolism and secondary wall deposition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and transcriptional activation of secondary wall components; proline biosynthesis/degradation balance; ROS-scavenging capacity; stomatal regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: POD genes; SOD genes; P5CS genes; PAL genes; CCoAOMT genes; 4CL genes; LAC genes; CCR; CESA genes; BpMYB61; BplMYB1; BplMYB2; BplMYB3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates stress-related and secondary wall biosynthetic genes including POD, SOD, P5CS, PAL, CCoAOMT, 4CL, LAC, CCR and CESA; positively regulates BpMYB61; indirectly represses/associates with lower expression of proline degradation genes P5CDH and ProDH and hemicellulose-related FRA and IRX
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation through binding MYBCORE and AC-box cis-elements in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYBCORE motif-containing promoters; AC-box motif-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salt stress (NaCl); osmotic stress (mannitol); ABA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced by NaCl, ABA and mannitol; highly expressed in stems, especially mature lower internodes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by NaCl, ABA and mannitol; highest in 18th stem internode and mature stem tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and GFP/LUC/GUS transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no obvious growth penalty under control conditions; altered wall composition with reduced hemicellulose may affect biomass processing or mechanical properties
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for engineering birch with improved salt/osmotic tolerance and higher lignin/cellulose content for forestry; caution that reduced hemicellulose may alter wood quality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BplMYB46 is a nuclear R2R3-MYB transcriptional activator induced by NaCl, ABA and mannitol and predominantly expressed in stems. 2. Overexpression enhances salt/osmotic tolerance by increasing SOD/POD activity, reducing ROS, promoting proline accumulation, reducing cell death and decreasing stomatal aperture/water loss. 3. BplMYB46 binds MYBCORE and AC-box motifs and directly activates promoters of stress-related and secondary wall genes. 4. Overexpression increases lignin deposition, fibre secondary wall thickness, lignin and cellulose content, while reducing hemicellulose content.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper focuses on stress tolerance and secondary wall deposition rather than classic nutritional quality traits; however, proline and wall biopolymer metabolism are the relevant metabolic outputs. ChIP evidence was generated using 35S:BplMYB46-GFP overexpression lines, which authors note may not fully reflect native occupancy.
